devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । ततः प्राचेतसोऽसिक्न्यामनुनीतः स्वयम्भुवा ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca tataḥ prācetaso

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: -31. (They are:) Dvimūrdhan, Śambara, Ariṣṭa, Hayagrīva, Vibhāvasu, Ayomukha, Śaṅkuśiras, Svarbhānu, Kapila, Aruṇa, Puloman, Vṛṣaparvan, Ekacakra, Anutāpana, Dhūmrakeśa, Virūpākṣa, Vipracitti and Durjaya.

devanagari: षष्टिं सञ्जनयामास दुहितॄः पितृवत्सलाः । दश धर्माय कायेन्दोर्द्विषट्त्रिणव दत्तवान् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: ṣaṣṭiṃ sañjanayāmāsa duhitṝḥ pitṛvatsalāḥ daśa dharmāya kāyendordviṣaṭtriṇava dattavān
translation: He gave ten daughters (in marriage) to Dharma, the presiding deity of righteousness, thirteen to sage Ka (Kaśyapa), twenty-seven to the Moon-god, two each to sages Bhūta, Aṅgiras and Kṛśāśva and the rest to Tārkṣya, another name of Kaśyapa.

devanagari: भूताङ्गिरः कृशाश्वेभ्यो द्वे द्वे तार्क्ष्याय चापराः । नामधेयान्यमूषां त्वं सापत्यानां च मे शृणु ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūtāṅgiraḥ kṛśāśvebhyo dve dve tārkṣyāya cāparāḥ nāmadheyānyamūṣāṃ tvaṃ sāpatyānāṃ ca me śṛṇu
translation: Now, hear from me their names along with those of their offspring whose children and children’s children (descendants) have populated completely the three worlds.

devanagari: यासां प्रसूतिप्रसवैर्लोका आपूरितास्त्रयः । भानुर्लम्बा ककुद्यामिर्विश्वा साध्या मरुत्वती ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: yāsāṃ prasūtiprasavairlokā āpūritāstrayaḥ bhānurlambā kakudyāmirviśvā sādhyā marutvatī

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ sādh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sādh class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ sādh class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ sādh class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ sādh class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ sādh class 5 verb]

[nominative plural from √ sādh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sādh class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ sādh class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ sādh class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ sādh class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ sādh class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ sādh class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ sādh class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ sādh class 5 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sidh ], [vocative plural from √ sidh ]
[nominative plural from √ sidh ], [vocative plural from √ sidh ], [accusative plural from √ sidh ]

translation: (The names of Dharma’s wives are): Bhānu, Lambā, Kakubh, Jāmi, Viśvā, Sādhyā, Marutvatī, Vasu, Muhūrtā, Saṅkalpā. Now listen to (the names of the sons).

devanagari: वसुर्मुहूर्ता सङ्कल्पा धर्मपत्न्यः सुताञ्शृणु । भानोस्तु देवऋषभ इन्द्र सेनस्ततो नृप ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: vasurmuhūrtā saṅkalpā dharmapatnyaḥ sutāñśṛṇu bhānostu devaṛṣabha indra senastato nṛpa
translation: From Bhānu was born Devaṛṣabha. His son was Indrasena. From Lambā was born Vidyota who gave birth to (deities presiding over) cloud.

devanagari: विद्योत आसील्लम्बायास्ततश्च स्तनयित्नवः । ककुदः सङ्कटस्तस्य कीकटस्तनयो यतः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: vidyota āsīllambāyāstataśca stanayitnavaḥ kakudaḥ saṅkaṭastasya kīkaṭastanayo yataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]
[accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Saṅkaṭa was born from Kakubh. His son was Kīkaṭa, from whom were begotten the spirits presiding over the fortresses on the earth. Svarga was the son of Jāmi. From him was born Nandi.

devanagari: भुवो दुर्गाणि यामेयः स्वर्गो नन्दिस्ततोऽभवत् । विश्वेदेवास्तु विश्वाया अप्रजांस्तान्प्रचक्षते ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: bhuvo durgāṇi yāmeyaḥ svargo nandistato viśvedevāstu viśvāyā aprajāṃstānpracakṣate
translation: From Viśva were begotten the Viśvedevas who are said to be childless. Sādhyā gave birth to a group of gods known as Sādhya whose son was Arthasiddhi.

devanagari: साध्योगणश्च साध्याया अर्थसिद्धिस्तु तत्सुतः । मरुत्वांश्च जयन्तश्च मरुत्वत्या बभूवतुः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: sādhyogaṇaśca sādhyāyā arthasiddhistu tatsutaḥ marutvāṃśca jayantaśca marutvatyā babhūvatuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [ablative single from √ sādh class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ sādh class 1 verb], [ablative single from √ sādh class 4 verb], [genitive single from √ sādh class 4 verb], [ablative single from √ sādh class 5 verb], [genitive single from √ sādh class 5 verb]
[ablative single from √ sidh ], [genitive single from √ sidh ]
[nominative plural from √ ji class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ ji class 1 verb]

translation: From Marutvatī were born (the forty-nine) Maruts (wind-gods) and Jayanta who was a part manifestation of Vāsudeva and whom they know as Upendra.

devanagari: जयन्तो वासुदेवांश उपेन्द्र इति यं विदुः । मौहूर्तिका देवगणा मुहूर्तायाश्च जज्ञिरे ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: jayanto vāsudevāṃśa upendra iti yaṃ viduḥ mauhūrtikā devagaṇā muhūrtāyāśca jajñire

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ ji class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ ji class 1 verb]

translation: From Muhūrtā were born the race of gods called Mauhūrtikas who verily bestow upon living beings fruit appropriate to the particular period of time (when an act is performed at that time).

devanagari: ये वै फलं प्रयच्छन्ति भूतानां स्वस्वकालजम् । सङ्कल्पायास्तु सङ्कल्पः कामः सङ्कल्पजः स्मृतः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: ye vai phalaṃ prayacchanti bhūtānāṃ svasvakālajam saṅkalpāyāstu saṅkalpaḥ kāmaḥ saṅkalpajaḥ smṛtaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative single from √ pre class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ pre class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ pre class 2 verb]

translation: Saṅkalpā gave birth to Saṅkalpa (the deity presiding over thought process). It is declared that the god of love is born of Saṅkalpa. Vasu gave birth to eight sons called Vasus. Hear from me their names (now).

devanagari: वसवोऽष्टौ वसोः पुत्रास्तेषां नामानि मे शृणु । द्रोणः प्राणो ध्रुवोऽर्कोऽग्निर्दोषो वास्तुर्विभावसुः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: vasavo droṇaḥ prāṇo dhruvo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ aś class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ aś class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ aś class 5 verb]

translation: (Their names are): Droṇa, Prāna, Dhruva, Arka, Agni, Doṣa, Vasu, Vibhāvasu. Droṇa begot from his wife Abhimati children called Harṣa, Śoka, Bhaya and others (the presiding deities of joy, sorrow, fear etc.).

devanagari: द्रोणस्याभिमतेः पत्न्या हर्षशोकभयादयः । प्राणस्योर्जस्वती भार्या सह आयुः पुरोजवः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: droṇasyābhimateḥ patnyā harṣaśokabhayādayaḥ prāṇasyorjasvatī bhāryā saha āyuḥ purojavaḥ
translation: Prāṇa begot Sahas, Āyus, Purojava from his wife Ūrjasvatī. Dharaṇī (the earth), the wife of Dhruva, gave births to various towns and cities.

devanagari: ध्रुवस्य भार्या धरणिरसूत विविधाः पुरः । अर्कस्य वासना भार्या पुत्रास्तर्षादयः स्मृताः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: dhruvasya bhāryā dharaṇirasūta vividhāḥ puraḥ arkasya vāsanā bhāryā putrāstarṣādayaḥ smṛtāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ bhṛ ], [vocative plural from √ bhṛ ]
[nominative plural from √ bhṛ ], [vocative plural from √ bhṛ ], [accusative plural from √ bhṛ ]
[nominative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb]

translation: Vāsanā was the wife of Arka (the Sun-god). Their sons like Tarṣa and others were well known. Dhārā was the wife of the Vasu called Agni, and their sons were Dravi- ṇaka and others.

devanagari: अग्नेर्भार्या वसोर्धारा पुत्रा द्रविणकादयः । स्कन्दश्च कृत्तिकापुत्रो ये विशाखादयस्ततः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: agnerbhāryā vasordhārā putrā draviṇakādayaḥ skandaśca kṛttikāputro ye viśākhādayastataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ bhṛ ], [vocative plural from √ bhṛ ]
[nominative plural from √ bhṛ ], [vocative plural from √ bhṛ ], [accusative plural from √ bhṛ ]

translation: And Skanda was the son of Kṛttikā (another wife of Agni). From him were born Viśākha and others. Doṣa had by Śarvarī, a son called Śiśumāra (the deity of the stellar sphere) who was an aṃśa (digit) of Hari.

devanagari: दोषस्य शर्वरीपुत्रः शिशुमारो हरेः कला । वास्तोराङ्गिरसीपुत्रो विश्वकर्माकृतीपतिः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: doṣasya śarvarīputraḥ śiśumāro hareḥ kalā vāstorāṅgirasīputro viśvakarmākṛtīpatiḥ
translation: From Aṅgirasī, the wife of Vasu, was born Viśvakarmā, the architect of gods. The name of his wife was Ākṛti. From her were born Cākṣusa, (the sixth) Manu. Viśvedevas and Sādhya gods were the sons of Manu.

devanagari: ततो मनुश्चाक्षुषोऽभूद्विश्वे साध्या मनोः सुताः । विभावसोरसूतोषा व्युष्टं रोचिषमातपम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: tato manuścākṣuṣo vibhāvasorasūtoṣā vyuṣṭaṃ rociṣamātapam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ sādh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sādh class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ sādh class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ sādh class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ sādh class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ sādh class 5 verb]

[nominative plural from √ sādh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sādh class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ sādh class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ sādh class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ sādh class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ sādh class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ sādh class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ sādh class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ sādh class 5 verb]

[nominative plural from √ sū class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ sū class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ vyuṣ class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ vyuṣ class 4 verb]

translation: From Vibhāvasu, Uṣā gave birth to Vyuṣṭa, Rociṣa and Ātapa. From Ātapa was born Pañcayāma (the deity presiding over the day-time) who keeps created beings awake and alert to their duties.

devanagari: पञ्चयामोऽथ भूतानि येन जाग्रति कर्मसु । सरूपासूत भूतस्य भार्या रुद्रांश्च कोटिशः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: pañcayāmo sarūpāsūta bhūtasya bhāryā rudrāṃśca koṭiśaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ jāgṛ class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ jāgṛ class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ jāgṛ class 2 verb], [locative single from √ jāgṛ class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ bhṛ ], [vocative plural from √ bhṛ ]
[nominative plural from √ bhṛ ], [vocative plural from √ bhṛ ], [accusative plural from √ bhṛ ]

translation: Sarūpā, the wife of Bhūta, gave birth to crores of Rudras (the deities entrusted with the duty of destruction), the chief among them were the eleven, namely, Raivata, Aja, Bhava, Bhīma, Vāma, Ugra, Vṛṣākapi. Ajaikapāda, Ahirbudhnya, Bahurūpa and Mahān. From Bhūtā, another wife of Bhūta, were born the terrible Bhūtas and evil-spirits (called Vināyakas) who were the attendants of Rudras.

devanagari: रैवतोऽजो भवो भीमो वाम उग्रो वृषाकपिः । अजैकपादहिर्बुध्न्यो बहुरूपो महानिति ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: raivato ajaikapādahirbudhnyo bahurūpo mahāniti
translation: Sarūpā, the wife of Bhūta, gave birth to crores of Rudras (the deities entrusted with the duty of destruction), the chief among them were the eleven, namely, Raivata, Aja, Bhava, Bhīma, Vāma, Ugra, Vṛṣākapi. Ajaikapāda, Ahirbudhnya, Bahurūpa and Mahān. From Bhūtā, another wife of Bhūta, were born the terrible Bhūtas and evil-spirits (called Vināyakas) who were the attendants of Rudras.

devanagari: रुद्रस्य पार्षदाश्चान्ये घोराः प्रेतविनायकाः । प्रजापतेरङ्गिरसः स्वधा पत्नी पितॄनथ ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: rudrasya pārṣadāścānye ghorāḥ pretavināyakāḥ prajāpateraṅgirasaḥ svadhā patnī pitṝnatha
translation: Svadhā, the wife of Prajāpati Aṅgiras, gave birth to the manes (Pitṛs). Satī, another wife of Aṅgiras, accepted the Veda called Atharva—Aṅgiras—as her son

devanagari: अथर्वाङ्गिरसं वेदं पुत्रत्वे चाकरोत्सती । कृशाश्वोऽर्चिषि भार्यायां धूमकेतुमजीजनत् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: atharvāṅgirasaṃ vedaṃ putratve cākarotsatī kṛśāśvo
translation: Krśāśva begot from his wife Arcis, Dhūmrakeśa as the son, and on Dhiṣaṇā four sons, viz. Vedaśiras, Devala, Vayuna and Manu.

devanagari: धिषणायां वेदशिरो देवलं वयुनं मनुम् । तार्क्ष्यस्य विनता कद्रूः पतङ्गी यामिनीति च ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: dhiṣaṇāyāṃ vedaśiro devalaṃ vayunaṃ manum tārkṣyasya vinatā kadrūḥ pataṅgī yāminīti ca
translation: Tārkṣya which was another name of the sage Kaśyapa, had four wives, namely, Vinatā, Kadrū, Pataṅgī and Yāminī. Pataṅgī gave birth to birds; Yāminī to locusts. Vinatā had a son called Garuḍa who was the vehicle of directly the Lord of Sacrifices (Viṣṇu). She had another son called Aruṇa (the thighless) who is the charioteer of the Sun-god. Kadrū, mothered serpents of various species.

devanagari: पतङ्ग्यसूत पतगान्यामिनी शलभानथ । सुपर्णासूत गरुडं साक्षाद्यज्ञेशवाहनम् । सूर्यसूतमनूरुं च कद्रूर्नागाननेकशः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: pataṅgyasūta patagānyāminī śalabhānatha suparṇāsūta garuḍaṃ sākṣādyajñeśavāhanam sūryasūtamanūruṃ ca kadrūrnāgānanekaśaḥ
translation: Tārkṣya which was another name of the sage Kaśyapa, had four wives, namely, Vinatā, Kadrū, Pataṅgī and Yāminī. Pataṅgī gave birth to birds; Yāminī to locusts. Vinatā had a son called Garuḍa who was the vehicle of directly the Lord of Sacrifices (Viṣṇu). She had another son called Aruṇa (the thighless) who is the charioteer of the Sun-god. Kadrū, mothered serpents of various species.

devanagari: कृत्तिकादीनि नक्षत्राणीन्दोः पत्न्यस्तु भारत । दक्षशापात्सोऽनपत्यस्तासु यक्ष्मग्रहार्दितः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛttikādīni nakṣatrāṇīndoḥ patnyastu bhārata dakṣaśāpātso
translation: Oh descendant of Bharata! The presiding deities of Kṛttikā and other twenty-seven constellations of stars, were wedded to the Moon, but due to the curse of Dakṣa, he was affected by consumption and had no issue from them.

devanagari: पुनः प्रसाद्य तं सोमः कला लेभे क्षये दिताः । शृणु नामानि लोकानां मातॄणां शङ्कराणि च ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: punaḥ prasādya taṃ somaḥ kalā lebhe kṣaye ditāḥ śṛṇu nāmāni lokānāṃ mātṝṇāṃ śaṅkarāṇi ca

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Having propitiated Dakṣa again, he regained his digits which decreased in the dark half of the month but had no children. Now, listen to the auspicious names of the mothers of the world (viz. Kaśyapa’s wives).

devanagari: अथ कश्यपपत्नीनां यत्प्रसूतमिदं जगत् । अदितिर्दितिर्दनुः काष्ठा अरिष्टा सुरसा इला ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: atha kaśyapapatnīnāṃ yatprasūtamidaṃ jagat aditirditirdanuḥ kāṣṭhā ariṣṭā surasā ilā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: The following are the names of the wives of Kaśyapa who gave birth to this world; Aditi, Diti, Danu, Kāṣṭhā, Ariṣṭā, Surasā, Ilā, Muni, Krodhavaśā, Tāmrā, Surabhi, Saramā and Timi. From Timi were born aquatic beings; from Saramā, tiger and other carnivorous animals.

devanagari: मुनिः क्रोधवशा ताम्रा सुरभिः सरमा तिमिः । तिमेर्यादोगणा आसन्श्वापदाः सरमासुताः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: muniḥ krodhavaśā tāmrā surabhiḥ saramā timiḥ timeryādogaṇā āsanśvāpadāḥ saramāsutāḥ
translation: The following are the names of the wives of Kaśyapa who gave birth to this world; Aditi, Diti, Danu, Kāṣṭhā, Ariṣṭā, Surasā, Ilā, Muni, Krodhavaśā, Tāmrā, Surabhi, Saramā and Timi. From Timi were born aquatic beings; from Saramā, tiger and other carnivorous animals.

devanagari: सुरभेर्महिषा गावो ये चान्ये द्विशफा नृप । ताम्रायाः श्येनगृध्राद्या मुनेरप्सरसां गणाः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: surabhermahiṣā gāvo ye cānye dviśaphā nṛpa tāmrāyāḥ śyenagṛdhrādyā munerapsarasāṃ gaṇāḥ
translation: From Surabhi, were created buffalo, cow and other beasts with cloven hooves; from Tāmrā, hawks, vultures and other birds of prey; from Muni, the bevy of celestial damsels.

devanagari: दन्दशूकादयः सर्पा राजन्क्रोधवशात्मजाः । इलाया भूरुहाः सर्वे यातुधानाश्च सौरसाः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: dandaśūkādayaḥ sarpā rājankrodhavaśātmajāḥ ilāyā bhūruhāḥ sarve yātudhānāśca saurasāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Oh King! Serpents like Dandaśūka and others were the offspring of Krodhavaśā; the vegetable kingdom, of Ilā, and the Yakṣas and Rakṣasas of Surasā.

devanagari: अरिष्टायास्तु गन्धर्वाः काष्ठाया द्विशफेतराः । सुता दनोरेकषष्टिस्तेषां प्राधानिकाञ्शृणु ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: ariṣṭāyāstu gandharvāḥ kāṣṭhāyā dviśaphetarāḥ sutā danorekaṣaṣṭisteṣāṃ prādhānikāñśṛṇu

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ su class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ su class 5 verb]
[nominative plural from √ su class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ su class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ su class 5 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation: The Gandharvas (celestial singers) were born of Ariṣṭā; beasts with uncloven feet (e.g. horses, donkeys etc.), of Kāṣṭhā; Danu had sixty-one sons. Listen to the names of the prominent ones in them:

devanagari: द्विमूर्धा शम्बरोऽरिष्टो हयग्रीवो विभावसुः । अयोमुखः शङ्कुशिराः स्वर्भानुः कपिलोऽरुणः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: dvimūrdhā śambaro ayomukhaḥ śaṅkuśirāḥ svarbhānuḥ kapilo

devanagari: पुलोमा वृषपर्वा च एकचक्रोऽनुतापनः । धूम्रकेशो विरूपाक्षो विप्रचित्तिश्च दुर्जयः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: pulomā vṛṣaparvā ca ekacakro dhūmrakeśo virūpākṣo vipracittiśca durjayaḥ

devanagari: स्वर्भानोः सुप्रभां कन्यामुवाह नमुचिः किल । वृषपर्वणस्तु शर्मिष्ठां ययातिर्नाहुषो बली ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: svarbhānoḥ suprabhāṃ kanyāmuvāha namuciḥ kila vṛṣaparvaṇastu śarmiṣṭhāṃ yayātirnāhuṣo balī

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: As is reported, Namuci married Suprabhā, the daughter of Svarbhānu. The mighty Yayāti, the son of Nahuṣa, was wedded to Śarmiṣṭhā, the daughter of Vṛṣaparvan.

devanagari: वैश्वानरसुतायाश्च चतस्रश्चारुदर्शनाः । उपदानवी हयशिरा पुलोमा कालका तथा ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: vaiśvānarasutāyāśca catasraścārudarśanāḥ upadānavī hayaśirā pulomā kālakā tathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [ablative single from √ su class 5 verb], [genitive single from √ su class 5 verb]
[ablative single from √ sū class 6 verb], [genitive single from √ sū class 6 verb]
[ablative single from √ sū class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation: Danu’s son Vaiśvānara had four daughters who were very beautiful to look at. They are: Upadānavī, Hayaśirā, Pulomā and Kālakā.

devanagari: उपदानवीं हिरण्याक्षः क्रतुर्हयशिरां नृप । पुलोमां कालकां च द्वे वैश्वानरसुते तु कः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: upadānavīṃ hiraṇyākṣaḥ kraturhayaśirāṃ nṛpa pulomāṃ kālakāṃ ca dve vaiśvānarasute tu kaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ su class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ su class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ su class 5 verb], [locative single from √ su class 5 verb]
[nominative dual from √ su class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ su class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ su class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ su class 5 verb]
[nominative dual from √ sū class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ sū class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ sū class 6 verb], [locative single from √ sū class 6 verb]

[nominative dual from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ sū class 2 verb], [locative single from √ sū class 2 verb]
[nominative dual from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation: Out of them, Upadānavī was married to Hiraṇyākṣa and Hayaśirā to Kratu. Oh King, Kaśyapa married Pulomā and Kālakā, the two daughters of Vaiśvānara.

devanagari: उपयेमेऽथ भगवान्कश्यपो ब्रह्मचोदितः । पौलोमाः कालकेयाश्च दानवा युद्धशालिनः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: upayeme paulomāḥ kālakeyāśca dānavā yuddhaśālinaḥ
translation: The great sage Kaśyapa married them as he was directed to do so by god Brahmā. The sons of Pulomā were the Dānavas known as Kālakeyas who were famous for their warlike nature.

devanagari: तयोः षष्टिसहस्राणि यज्ञघ्नांस्ते पितुः पिता । जघान स्वर्गतो राजन्नेक इन्द्र प्रियङ्करः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: tayoḥ ṣaṣṭisahasrāṇi yajñaghnāṃste pituḥ pitā jaghāna svargato rājanneka indra priyaṅkaraḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Out of them, sixty-thousand sons known as Nivātakavacas who used to disturb sacrifices, were killed by your father’s father single-handedly, while he paid a visit to Svarga and wanted to please Indra.

devanagari: विप्रचित्तिः सिंहिकायां शतं चैकमजीजनत् । राहुज्येष्ठं केतुशतं ग्रहत्वं य उपागताः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: vipracittiḥ siṃhikāyāṃ śataṃ caikamajījanat rāhujyeṣṭhaṃ ketuśataṃ grahatvaṃ ya upāgatāḥ
translation: Vipracitti begot on his wife Siṃhikā one hundred and one sons. The eldest of them was Rāhu who got a position in the planetary system, along with the remaining hundred sons who were named Ketu.

devanagari: अथातः श्रूयतां वंशो योऽदितेरनुपूर्वशः । यत्र नारायणो देवः स्वांशेनावातरद्विभुः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: athātaḥ śrūyatāṃ vaṃśo yo yatra nārāyaṇo devaḥ svāṃśenāvātaradvibhuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: Now, listen from me in due sequence, the details of the race of Aditi wherein the Almighty God Nārāyaṇa incarnated by his own aṃśa. (digit).

devanagari: विवस्वानर्यमा पूषा त्वष्टाथ सविता भगः । धाता विधाता वरुणो मित्रः शत्रु उरुक्रमः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: vivasvānaryamā pūṣā tvaṣṭātha savitā bhagaḥ dhātā vidhātā varuṇo mitraḥ śatru urukramaḥ
translation: The sons of Aditi were: Vivasvān, Aryaman, Pūṣan, Tvaṣṭṛ, Savitṛ, Bhaga, Dhātṛ, Vidhātṛ, Varuṇa, Mitra, Indra and Trivikrama (Vāmana). (These are the presiding deities of the orb of the Sun each month).

devanagari: विवस्वतः श्राद्धदेवं संज्ञासूयत वै मनुम् । मिथुनं च महाभागा यमं देवं यमीं तथा । सैव भूत्वाथ वडवा नासत्यौ सुषुवे भुवि ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: vivasvataḥ śrāddhadevaṃ saṃjñāsūyata vai manum mithunaṃ ca mahābhāgā yamaṃ devaṃ yamīṃ tathā saiva bhūtvātha vaḍavā nāsatyau suṣuve bhuvi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: The highly fortunate Saṃjñā, the wife of Vivasvat, gave birth to Śrāddhadeva, the Maṅu and a twin called the Yama god and (his sister) Yamī. That very Saṃjñā became a female horse and gave birth to the twin gods Aśvinīkumāras, on this earth.

devanagari: छाया शनैश्चरं लेभे सावर्णिं च मनुं ततः । कन्यां च तपतीं या वै वव्रे संवरणं पतिम् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: chāyā śanaiścaraṃ lebhe sāvarṇiṃ ca manuṃ tataḥ kanyāṃ ca tapatīṃ yā vai vavre saṃvaraṇaṃ patim

[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Chāyā (another wife of Vivasvat) gave birth to Śanaiścara, the presiding deity of the planet Saturn, and Sāvarṇi Manu. She gave birth to a daughter also called Tapatī who chose in marriage Saṃvaraṇa as her husband.

devanagari: अर्यम्णो मातृका पत्नी तयोश्चर्षणयः सुताः । यत्र वै मानुषी जातिर्ब्रह्मणा चोपकल्पिता ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: aryamṇo mātṛkā patnī tayoścarṣaṇayaḥ sutāḥ yatra vai mānuṣī jātirbrahmaṇā copakalpitā

translation: Mātṛkā was the wife of Aryaman. Their sons were called Garṣaṇi (for they possessed wisdom of what should and what should not be done). From them were evolved the human kind by god Brahmā.

devanagari: पूषानपत्यः पिष्टादो भग्नदन्तोऽभवत्पुरा । योऽसौ दक्षाय कुपितं जहास विवृतद्विजः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: pūṣānapatyaḥ piṣṭādo bhagnadanto yo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ kup class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ kup class 10 verb]

translation: Pūṣan was childless. He got his teeth broken formerly as he exhibited his teeth in laughing at Śiva when he was enraged at Dakṣa, and Pūṣan had to subsist on flour.

devanagari: त्वष्टुर्दैत्यात्मजा भार्या रचना नाम कन्यका । सन्निवेशस्तयोर्जज्ञे विश्वरूपश्च वीर्यवान् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: tvaṣṭurdaityātmajā bhāryā racanā nāma kanyakā sanniveśastayorjajñe viśvarūpaśca vīryavān

translation: The girl Racanā, the younger sister of the Daityas, became the spouse of Tvaṣṭṛ. From them were born sons namely Saṃniveśa and the mighty Viśvarūpa.

devanagari: तं वव्रिरे सुरगणा स्वस्रीयं द्विषतामपि । विमतेन परित्यक्ता गुरुणाङ्गिरसेन यत् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ vavrire suragaṇā svasrīyaṃ dviṣatāmapi vimatena parityaktā guruṇāṅgirasena yat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: The host of gods selected him (for preceptorship) even though he was the nephew (sister’s son) of their enemies, as they were forsaken by their preceptor Bṛhaspati who was insulted by them.
